headline: In one month of war, oil production has plunged
description: A quarter of the world's seaborne crude oil trade passed through the Strait of Hormuz in 2025. Since the start of the war in the Middle East, Gulf oil-producing countries have reduced their output by between 25% and 80%, depending on the case, because they have been unable to export.

headline: Iran warns of 'crushing' attacks on US after Trump threats
Trump fires Pam Bondi as US attorney general
Sarkozy appeal trial hears moving testimonies from families of UTA flight bombing victims
description: On Wednesday, 13 relatives of victims killed in the 1989 plane bombing took to the witness stand to express their anger at former French president Nicolas Sarkozy. He is accused of making a pact with the Libyan masterminds of the bombing to receive campaign funding.

headline: TotalEnergies traders reap unprecedented profits off Middle East war
description: The French major's oil-trading subsidiary made a particularly lucrative move by speculating on a closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: 'With Céline Dion's Paris shows, the city becomes a spin-off product of mega-concerts'
description: In an op-ed, Rémy Knafou, the emeritus professor at Université Panthéon-Sorbonne, argues that Dion's concert series in Paris is a textbook example of overtourism, where economic interests, environmental concerns, the ambiguous role of public services and pure commercial promotion all intersect.

headline: 'Refusing to divide French society into the racialized and the non-racialized does not mean denying the existence of racism'
description: Sociologist Stéphane Beaud and historian Gérard Noiriel analyze why people who have experienced racism – such as Bally Bagayoko, Saint-Denis's new mayor – may reject the label 'racialized.'

French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
description: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

headline: The deadly cheetah trafficking between Somaliland and the Gulf
description: The feline is highly sought after by wealthy collectors of wild animals, who are numerous on the Arabian Peninsula. Across the way, in the Horn of Africa, Somaliland has become the main hub of a thriving trafficking network, even as the species itself is under threat.

headline: Céline Dion picks Paris for her return to the stage after a six-year hiatus
description: After several days of hints, the Canadian star waited for her birthday to announce her first concert dates since 2020: She will be in residence for 10 nights at Paris La Défense Arena, between September 12 and October 14.
